section.title: Overview
section.para: The rust_refer_handler module tracks &sip; REFER transactions and their associated NOTIFY messages to provide a complete state machine for call transfer tracking. The module maintains per-worker transfer state keyed by Call-ID.

Key features include:
Blind and attended transfer tracking with Replaces header parsing.
Automatic REFER/NOTIFY interception via dialog callbacks (auto_process mode).
NOTIFY generation with sipfrag status and CSeq tracking.
Transfer target policy enforcement via glob-pattern allow lists.
Transfer lifecycle event publishing for external integrations.
Transfer timeout detection with optional reconnect of original parties.

section.title: How it works
section.para:
The module implements a REFER/NOTIFY state machine with four states:
pending - REFER received, no NOTIFY yet.
trying - NOTIFY with 100 Trying received.
success - NOTIFY with 2xx status received.
failed - NOTIFY with 3xx-6xx status received.
Terminal states (success, failed) are sticky: once a transfer reaches a terminal state, subsequent NOTIFY messages do not change the status. The notify_count is still incremented.

In manual mode (default), the script is responsible for calling handle_refer() and handle_notify() explicitly. In auto_process mode, the module registers dialog callbacks to automatically intercept in-dialog REFER and NOTIFY messages.
For attended transfers, the handle_attended_refer() function parses the Replaces header to extract the consultation call leg information (call-id, from-tag, to-tag).

Stale REFER state is automatically expired after expire_secs. When the number of tracked transfers exceeds max_pending, an automatic sweep removes expired entries. Transfers that exceed transfer_timeout_secs without reaching a terminal state are marked as failed.

section.title: &osips; Modules
section.para:
The following modules must be loaded before this module:
dialog - Required only when auto_process is set to 1.

section.title: External Libraries or Applications
section.para:
The following libraries or applications must be installed before running &osips; with this module loaded:
None.

section.title: max_pending (integer)
section.para: Maximum number of REFER transactions tracked per worker process. When this limit is reached, a sweep is triggered to remove expired entries. Must be at least 1; values below 1 are clamped to 1 at module load.

section.title: expire_secs (integer)
section.para: Time in seconds after which a tracked REFER transaction is considered stale and eligible for automatic removal. Must be at least 1; values below 1 are clamped to 1 at module load.

section.title: auto_process (integer)
section.para: When set to 1, the module registers dialog callbacks (DLGCB_CREATED and DLGCB_REQ_WITHIN) to automatically intercept in-dialog REFER and NOTIFY messages. This eliminates the need for explicit handle_refer() and handle_notify() calls in the script.
Requires the dialog module to be loaded. If dialog.so is not available and auto_process is set to 1, the module will fail to initialize.

section.title: allowed_targets (string)
section.para: Comma-separated list of glob patterns that define which transfer targets are allowed. If set, only REFER requests whose Refer-To value matches at least one pattern will be accepted. Patterns support * (matches zero or more characters) and ? (matches exactly one character). Matching is case-insensitive.
If not set or empty, all transfer targets are allowed.
This policy is enforced by both handle_refer(), handle_attended_refer(), and check_transfer_target().

para.emphasis: Default value is not set (all targets allowed).

section.title: publish_events (integer)
section.para: When set to 1, the module publishes JSON events on transfer lifecycle changes (start, success, failure). The events are tracked via the events_published statistic counter.

The JSON payload includes:
call_id - The Call-ID of the transfer.
refer_to - The transfer target.
status - The transfer status (pending, trying, success, failed).

section.title: transfer_timeout_secs (integer)
section.para: Maximum time in seconds to wait for a REFER-initiated call to reach a terminal state (success or failed). If the timeout is exceeded, the transfer is automatically marked as failed.

section.title: reconnect_on_failure (integer)
section.para: When set to 1 and a transfer times out or fails, the module will signal that the original parties should be reconnected. The reconnect intent is available via the transfer status (failed + reconnect_on_failure flag) for the script to act upon.
This parameter works in conjunction with transfer_timeout_secs.
Record a new blind REFER transaction. The Call-ID is automatically extracted from the &sip; message. The transfer state is initialized to pending.
If a REFER for the same Call-ID already exists, it is overwritten with fresh state.
If allowed_targets is configured, the refer_to value is checked against the pattern list. If the target does not match, the function returns -1 (denied).
Meaning of the parameters is as follows:

listitem.para: refer_to (string) - The value of the Refer-To header. Typically $hdr(Refer-To). This parameter is mandatory.

section.para: Return codes:

listitem.para: 1 - REFER recorded successfully.
-1 (denied) - target not in allowed_targets list.
-2 (error) - missing parameter, no Call-ID in message, or uninitialized worker.

section.para: This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE, FAILURE_ROUTE, and ONREPLY_ROUTE.

example.title: handle_refer usage

section.para: Record a new attended REFER transaction with a Replaces header. The Call-ID is automatically extracted from the &sip; message. The Replaces header value is parsed to extract the consultation call leg (call-id, from-tag, to-tag) and stored with the transfer state. The parsed Replaces value is also set in $var(refer_replaces).
If the Replaces header cannot be parsed (empty or malformed), the transfer is still tracked but the function returns -1.
Meaning of the parameters is as follows:

listitem.para: refer_to (string) - The value of the Refer-To header. This parameter is mandatory.
replaces (string) - The value of the Replaces header. Typically $hdr(Replaces). This parameter is mandatory.

section.para: Return codes:

listitem.para: 1 - attended REFER recorded with valid Replaces header.
-1 (denied) - Replaces header could not be parsed, or target not in allowed_targets.
-2 (error) - missing parameter, no Call-ID, or uninitialized worker.

section.para: This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE, FAILURE_ROUTE, and ONREPLY_ROUTE.

example.title: handle_attended_refer usage

section.para:
Process a NOTIFY message for a tracked REFER transaction. Updates the state machine based on the sipfrag status code:
100 -> trying
200-299 -> success (terminal)
300-699 -> failed (terminal)
When publish_events is enabled, a JSON event is generated for terminal state transitions (success or failed).
Meaning of the parameters is as follows:

listitem.para: call_id (string) - The Call-ID of the dialog the NOTIFY belongs to. Typically $ci. This parameter is mandatory.
status_code (string) - The &sip; status code from the message/sipfrag body. Parsed as an integer internally. This parameter is mandatory.

section.para: Return codes:

listitem.para: 1 - NOTIFY processed (matching REFER found).
-1 (denied) - no matching REFER found for this Call-ID.
-2 (error) - missing parameter or uninitialized worker.

section.para: This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE, FAILURE_ROUTE, and ONREPLY_ROUTE.

example.title: handle_notify usage

section.para: Query the current status of a tracked REFER transaction. Sets $var(refer_status) to one of: pending, trying, success, failed, or unknown (if no matching REFER is found).
Meaning of the parameters is as follows:

listitem.para: call_id (string) - The Call-ID to query. Typically $ci. This parameter is mandatory.

section.para: Return codes:

listitem.para: -1 (denied) - no matching REFER found; $var(refer_status) set to unknown.
-2 (error) - missing parameter or uninitialized worker.

section.para: This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE, FAILURE_ROUTE, and ONREPLY_ROUTE.

example.title: refer_status usage

section.para: Prepare a NOTIFY message with sipfrag status for a tracked REFER transaction. Sets pseudo-variables that the script can use to send the NOTIFY via t_uac_dlg() or other means.
The function tracks CSeq increments per call_id to ensure each NOTIFY has a unique, monotonically increasing CSeq value.
The following pseudo-variables are set:

listitem.para: $var(refer_notify_cseq) - The CSeq number for this NOTIFY.
$var(refer_notify_body) - The sipfrag body (e.g., SIP/2.0 200 OK).
$var(refer_notify_status) - The raw status code as a string.

section.para: Meaning of the parameters is as follows:

listitem.para: call_id (string) - The Call-ID of the tracked REFER. This parameter is mandatory.
status_code (string) - The sipfrag status code to include in the NOTIFY body. This parameter is mandatory.

section.para: Return codes:

listitem.para: 1 - NOTIFY prepared successfully.
-2 (error) - missing parameter or invalid status code.

section.para: This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE, FAILURE_ROUTE, and ONREPLY_ROUTE.

example.title: send_refer_notify usage

section.para: Check if a transfer target is allowed according to the allowed_targets configuration. This function can be used independently of handle_refer() for custom policy enforcement.
Meaning of the parameters is as follows:

listitem.para: refer_to (string) - The transfer target URI to check. This parameter is mandatory.

section.para: Return codes:

listitem.para: 1 - target is allowed.
-1 (denied) - target is blocked.
-2 (error) - missing parameter or uninitialized worker.

section.para: This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE, FAILURE_ROUTE, and ONREPLY_ROUTE.

example.title: check_transfer_target usage

section.para: Retrieve module statistics as a JSON string. Sets $var(refer_stats) with a JSON object containing all tracked counters for the current worker process.
Return codes:

listitem.para: 1 - always succeeds.

section.para: This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE, FAILURE_ROUTE, and ONREPLY_ROUTE.

section.title: $var(refer_status)
section.para:
The current status of a tracked REFER transaction. Set by refer_status(). Possible values:
pending - REFER received, awaiting NOTIFY.
trying - 100 Trying received.
success - 2xx received (transfer succeeded).
failed - 3xx-6xx received (transfer failed).
unknown - no matching REFER found.

section.title: $var(refer_replaces)
section.para: The raw Replaces header value from an attended transfer. Set by handle_attended_refer(). Contains the full Replaces header value including call-id, from-tag, and to-tag parameters.

section.title: $var(refer_notify_cseq)
section.para: The CSeq number for the next NOTIFY to send. Set by send_refer_notify(). Monotonically increasing per call_id.

section.title: $var(refer_notify_body)
section.para: The sipfrag body for a NOTIFY message. Set by send_refer_notify(). Contains a SIP status line, e.g., SIP/2.0 200 OK.

section.title: $var(refer_notify_status)
section.para: The raw status code string for a NOTIFY message. Set by send_refer_notify().

section.title: $var(refer_stats)
section.para: JSON string containing the module statistics for the current worker process. Set by refer_stats().
Number of currently tracked REFER transactions (not expired).
Number of REFER transactions that reached success state.
Number of REFER transactions that reached failed state.
Number of REFER transactions that were swept due to expiry.
Number of NOTIFY messages received with no matching REFER.
Number of REFER transactions that exceeded transfer_timeout_secs and were automatically marked as failed.
Number of transfer lifecycle events that were published (when publish_events is enabled).
